description:
Keeper Commander is a python-based CLI and SDK interface to the Keeper Security platform. Provides administrative controls, reporting, import/export and vault management.

Keeper Commander is a command-line and SDK interface to Keeper® Password Manager. Commander can be used to access and control your Keeper vault, perform administrative functions (such as end-user onboarding and data import/export), launch remote sessions, rotate passwords, eliminate hardcoded passwords and more. Keeper Commander is an open source project with contributions from Keeper's engineering team and partners.
Keeper Commander 18.0.1
Breaking changes
- Dropped Python 3.7 support. Minimum supported Python is now 3.8
New commands & features
SSO / Identity
- New sso-cloud command suite for managing Keeper SSO Connect Cloud configurations.
Cloud Secrets
- New cloud secrets import commands for importing secrets from external cloud providers.
KeeperPAM
- New pam connection jit command for managing Just-In-Time (JIT) settings on PAM resource records.
- New --proxy flag for pam tunnel start to enable KeeperDB Proxy mode.
- New saas profile option for pam rotation edit.
- pam launch now available from SuperShell.
- pam tunnel diagnose gains TURN/STUN end-to-end probes and per-test flags; log noise reduced.
- pam project import / extend now supports defining workflows.
Enterprise policy
- Implemented enforcement of Record Password and Record Types policies.
- shared-manager role label renamed to share-manager.
Other
- Password strength now graded via zxcvbn.
- PAM Workflow improvements and workflow-window session hard close.
- Rotation skip feature added (cp-rotation-skip-feat).
- KeeperDrive search command updated.
Fixes & Improvements
- Tunnel: SO_REUSEADDR disabled on tunnel start (prevents binding conflicts).
- PAM record update: added validations on update path.
- KeeperDrive: fixed role labels, kd-shortcut titles, removed kd-rndir inherit flags.
- Service mode: legacy service-config record titles handled gracefully on startup.
- Login UX: correct login --server hint shown for batch vs. shell mode.
